title: Your Pencil Has Been Lying To You

format.note: DURATION_IN_FRAMES + scene start frames are set from the real per-word timestamps in pencil_words.json once narration is rendered. The frames below are PLACEHOLDER estimates so the plan is buildable before timing exists.

energy_doctrine: FAST snappy in-composition motion (spring pops via usePop damping~120 mass~0.55, fast spins, fills, slices, conveyor) NOT camera zoom pulses. 8-frame scene fade in/out. 3-word karaoke captions are automatic.
palette_rationale: Warm woodshop/factory dark backdrop (cedar + graphite world). Graphite = cool slate grey to KILL the 'lead' myth on screen; clay = earthy tan; cedar = warm orange-brown; the payoff pencil = the iconic #FFC21E yellow with a pink eraser. Gold accent for labels/reveals.
shared_components: Svg/Defs wrapper with glow, soft, oven-glow filters and gradients: graphiteG (slate), clayG (tan), cedarG (woodgrain orange), yellowG (pencil paint), metalG (ferrule)
usePop(delay, damping=120) snappy overshoot spring; useEnter(delay) gentle spring for hero text
Background: warm dark gradient + drifting sawdust/graphite-dust specks (32) + warmGlow radial
Captions: 3-word karaoke pages, active word -> gold + scale 1.13, bottom 250

scenes.beat: Your pencil has been lying to you — there is no lead inside it at all.
scenes.narration_sentence: Your pencil has been lying to you, because there's no lead inside it at all.
scenes.visual: A big classic yellow #2 pencil pops in center-screen, tilted, hero. A magnifier lens slides over its sharpened tip; inside the lens the dark core is X-rayed to a cross-section. A bold strikethrough crosses the word 'LEAD' on a tag pinned to the core, and it morphs/relabels to 'GRAPHITE'. A red '!' warning badge pops at the eraser end. The pencil gives a tiny nervous wobble.
draw: Pencil body: long rounded hex prism in yellow (yellowG), painted ferrule (metalG) + pink eraser cap (eraser), sharpened cedar cone tip (cedarG) with a dark graphite point
Magnifier: ring (metalG stroke) + faint glass fill + handle, sweeps from off-screen onto the tip on usePop
Cross-section disc inside the lens: concentric — cedar ring, then solid graphite core (graphite, NOT shiny metal)
Myth tag: rounded pill reading 'LEAD' in red, a strikethrough line wipes across it, then text crossfades to 'GRAPHITE' in gold
Warning badge: red circle + white '!' (HotDog SceneHook pattern)
scenes.animations: Pencil springs in from scale 0.4 with overshoot (usePop damping 110). Lens slides in x over ~18f. Cross-section atoms/rings fade up staggered. Strikethrough draws across 'LEAD' (interpolate width 0->tagWidth) then label morphs to 'GRAPHITE' via opacity crossfade + gold pop. '!' badge pops at f~40. Pencil nervous wobble rotate 2.2*sin(f/2.4) after the reveal.
scenes.sfx: soft whoosh as pencil pops in; a dramatic glass/chime DING on the 'GRAPHITE not LEAD' tag flip (this is reveal #1)

scenes.beat: First: black graphite mineral is crushed to fine powder and mixed with wet clay.
scenes.narration_sentence: First, a black mineral called graphite is crushed into fine powder and mixed with wet clay.
scenes.label: Graphite + wet clay
scenes.visual: A chunky black graphite rock drops into a steel crusher/mortar; a piston/roller slams down (fast) and shatters it into a falling shower of fine grey powder that piles into a mixing bowl. A spout pours brown wet clay (clayWet) into the same bowl; a paddle spins fast, swirling powder+clay into a smooth grey-brown paste. A tiny '+CLAY' tag pops.
draw: Graphite rock: faceted polygon (graphite) with lighter facet highlights, labeled chip 'GRAPHITE'
Crusher: steel jaw / piston (metalG) that slams down with a squash on impact
Powder shower: ~16 small grey specks falling + accumulating pile (animated count)
Clay pour: a curved stream from a tilted jug (clayWet) filling the bowl, glossy wet highlight
Mixing bowl + fast paddle: bowl (dark steel), paddle spins f*9, paste morphs graphite->paste color as it blends
scenes.animations: Rock falls + crusher SLAMS (squash/stretch, screen-shake-free, just a quick scale punch on impact at f~26). Powder bursts outward then rains down. Clay stream draws in. Paddle spins fast; bowl fill color interpolates from chunky grey to smooth paste over 60f (morph 0->1). '+CLAY' tag usePop.
scenes.sfx: crunch/crush impact thud on the crusher slam; light liquid pour glug on the clay; no big boom (save those)

scenes.beat: Then the paste is squeezed through a tiny hole, forming long spaghetti-like strands.
scenes.narration_sentence: Then this paste is squeezed through a tiny hole, forming long spaghetti-like strands.
scenes.label: Squeezed like spaghetti
scenes.visual: A pressurized extruder cylinder (syringe/press) on the left squeezes the grey paste through a tiny circular die. Out the hole emerge long wiggling spaghetti-like graphite strands that snake rightward across the screen, wobbling like noodles, and coil/drape onto a tray. A zoomed callout circle shows the 'tiny hole' die face. A piston pushes in to drive the extrusion.
draw: Extruder body: horizontal steel cylinder (metalG) + plunger that advances left->right
Die plate: small dark plate with one tiny bright hole (the 'tiny hole'), callout ring + arrow 'tiny die'
Strands: 3-4 long sine-wave paths (graphite color) using a sinePath-style helper, animated phase so they wiggle/extrude outward
Drape tray: strands coil onto a tray at the right; a soft pile builds
scenes.animations: Plunger advances (interpolate x) shrinking the paste reservoir. Strands GROW from the die — path length animates outward (extend end-point) with a live sine wobble (noodle jiggle). Callout ring pops (usePop) zooming the die. Strands sway continuously.
scenes.sfx: wet squish/extrude squelch as strands emerge; subtle mechanical whir under it

scenes.beat: These strands are baked in an oven until they turn hard and smooth.
scenes.narration_sentence: And these strands are baked in an oven until they turn hard and smooth.
scenes.label: Baked rock-hard
scenes.visual: A glowing kiln/oven: the strands lie on a rack inside; heat waves shimmer and the interior pulses orange (ovenGlow). The strands visibly transform — dull soft grey -> darker, glossy, hard graphite with a sharp specular highlight sweeping along them. A temperature gauge needle swings up fast into the red. Heat-shimmer ripples rise.
draw: Oven: dark chamber box (metalG frame) with a glowing element bar at the bottom, internal radial ovenGlow
Rack of strands: 4-5 horizontal graphite rods on a wire rack
Heat shimmer: rising translucent wavy lines (sine) + soft orange glow ramp
Specular sweep: a white highlight bar sweeps across each rod as it 'hardens' (smooth/glossy)
Temp gauge: semicircle dial + needle that springs from cold to hot-red, plus 'HARD + SMOOTH' tag
scenes.animations: Oven glow ramps up (interpolate brightness 0->1). Strands color-morph dull->glossy graphite (mix over 70f). Specular highlight sweeps L->R repeatedly. Gauge needle springs into red (usePop). Heat-wave lines scroll upward. Tag 'HARD + SMOOTH' pops at end.
scenes.sfx: low oven 'whoomph' ignite + sizzling ambience; a satisfying glassy 'ting' when the strand turns glossy/hard (mini reveal)

scenes.beat: Next: strands are laid into grooves cut in a cedar slab, and a second slab is glued on top.
scenes.narration_sentence: Next, they're laid into grooves cut in a slab of cedar wood, and a second slab is glued on top.
scenes.label: Cedar wood sandwich
scenes.visual: Cross-section + top view of a cedar plank (cedarG woodgrain) with several parallel half-round GROOVES routed into it. Graphite rods drop one-by-one snapping into each groove. A glue line draws across, then a SECOND cedar slab swings down from above and clamps on top — sealing the rods inside (a literal pencil sandwich). A side cross-section shows the rod hugged by wood top+bottom.
draw: Bottom slab: long cedar board (cedarG) with visible woodgrain stroke lines + 5 routed semicircular grooves
Graphite rods: 5 rods that fall/snap into the grooves (staggered usePop)
Glue bead: dashed/solid bead line drawn along the slab top (white-ish), tiny glue-drip dots
Top slab: matching cedar board that hinges/swings down (rotate from above) and presses flat, with a squash on contact
Cross-section inset: circle showing cedar | graphite core | cedar, label 'sealed in wood'
scenes.animations: Grooves appear (router pass wipes them in). Rods drop+snap one by one (staggered springs, small bounce). Glue bead draws across (width interpolate). Top slab swings down (rotate -> 0) and clamps with a settle-squash. Cross-section inset pops to confirm the sandwich.
scenes.sfx: wood clatter as rods seat; a clean 'clack' wood-on-wood when the top slab clamps shut

scenes.beat: Finally the block is sliced into pencils, painted yellow, and tipped with an eraser nobody asked for.
scenes.narration_sentence: Finally, the wood is sliced into individual pencils, painted bright yellow, and tipped with an eraser nobody actually asked for.
scenes.visual: The glued cedar block runs under a fast circular saw / blade gang that slices it into individual hex pencils (the slabbed block fans apart into separate pencils). The bare wood pencils ride a conveyor through a paint curtain that coats them BRIGHT YELLOW (sweep fill). At the end a ferrule + pink eraser snaps onto each tip. The hero finished yellow pencil lands center for the payoff line, with a small wry callout on the eraser 'nobody asked for this'.
draw: Saw: spinning circular blade(s) (metalG, fast rotate) descending across the block; cut lines split the block into ~6 hex pencils that separate
Conveyor: ticking belt carrying bare pencils L->R
Paint curtain: a vertical falling sheet of yellow; pencils pass under and color-fill bare->yellow (clip wipe)
Ferrule+eraser assembly: metal band + pink eraser cap that snaps onto the tip (usePop)
Hero pencil: final iconic yellow #2 pencil, centered, plus payoff text and a sly arrow tag on the eraser
scenes.animations: Blade spins FAST and drops; block splits into pencils that slide apart (staggered x). Conveyor ticks scroll. Paint curtain falls; pencils fill yellow via a wipe-clip as they cross it (bare->yellow). Eraser snaps on with overshoot. Hero pencil springs to center; payoff line rises (useEnter). Eraser callout pops last.
scenes.sfx: zippy saw whir + slice 'shhk' on the cut; a final bright success CHIME / sting on the finished hero pencil (reveal #2 / payoff)

payoff: It was never lead. Just baked rock dust, hugged in wood — and a pink eraser nobody asked for.

payoff_onscreen.style: line2 big bold graphite-grey word 'ROCK', line3 in gold, wry tone matching the transcript's last clause

sfx_discipline: Sparse + scene-matched. Whoosh/slide on every scene change. Only TWO dramatic stingers: (1) the 'GRAPHITE not LEAD' tag-flip chime in the hook, (2) the finished-pencil success chime in the payoff. Process scenes use small diegetic sounds (crush, squish, oven whoomph, wood clack, saw shhk) — no boomy stacking.
build_notes: One scene per narration sentence: 6 scenes total (hook + 4 process steps + payoff).

Replace placeholder start_frame / duration_frames_est + DURATION_IN_FRAMES with real values from pencil_words.json sentence-end timestamps (multiply seconds * 30), exactly like SkyBlue/Mirror/HotDog set their S = {...} object.
Graphite must read as SLATE GREY, never silver/metal — the whole point is debunking 'lead'. Keep the pencil-yellow ONLY for the finished pencil so the payoff color-pops.
Reuse HotDog's usePop snappy spring + conveyor + SceneWrap cadence; reuse Mirror/SkyBlue cross-section + glow defs patterns.
Captions component is identical to the references (3-word pages) — do not author captions here.
